scenario: Quanta Telco's Camunda 8.8 self-service portal renders a Select dropdown for plan choice on 18000 user tasks per day, with the option list provided by an input-data options source that fetches the catalogue from a connector. The team wants the dropdown to pre-select STANDARD when no input value exists, and the developer asks why the Default value field is greyed out in the Select properties panel.
question: Why is the Default value field greyed out for that Camunda 8.8 Select configuration?

options.text: The Default value field is only available when the Select is configured with a static options source, and this Select uses an input-data options source instead of a static one
The Default value field is only available when the Select is marked Required, and the team has not enabled the Required validation entry on this Select in the panel
The Default value field is only available when the Select has Searchable disabled, and this configuration has the Searchable toggle enabled in the properties panel
The Default value field is only available when the Key is bound to a string variable, and the team has bound this Select to a variable typed as any in the panel

a.text: Correct. The Configurable properties section explicitly states that Default value is only available for static options sources, so input-data and expression-driven options sources disable the field in the properties panel of the Select.

b.text: Incorrect. The availability of the Default value field is not tied to the Required validation toggle; the documented gating is whether the options source is static.

c.text: Incorrect. The Searchable property controls keyboard search behaviour and does not gate the availability of the Default value field in the properties panel.

d.text: Incorrect. The Key binding accepts any data per the Datatypes section; the Default value gating is driven by the options source kind, not by the bound variable type.

explanation: Default value on a Select is only exposed when the options source is static. Input-data or expression-driven options sources hide the Default value field, since the runtime cannot guarantee a stable option list at design time against which to pre-select a value.
